citations.desc: Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors).
citations.narrative:
There was no written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) available for review at the time of the relicense inspection.
Although the applicant did have a formal arrangement with a part-time veterinarian who was the Attending Veterinarian
(AV) and animals have been receiving veterinary care, this veterinarian is no longer willing to function as the AV.
Per this Section, each dealer shall have an Attending Veterinarian (AV) who shall provide adequate veterinary care to its
animals in compliance with this section and the dealer shall employ an AV under formal arrangements. In the case of a
part-time Attending Veterinarian or consultant arrangements, the formal arrangements shall include a written Program of
Veterinary Care and regularly scheduled visits to the premises of the dealer. The applicant needs to establish formal
arrangements with a veterinarian who will serve as the AV and provide a PVC.
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “B” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the
applicant.
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection(s) or by 7/4/26 (60 days counted from 1st RL Inspection) or the
applicant will forfeit the license fee and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your
next Re-License Inspection.
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires on 6/5/26.
n

narrative:
2.40(a)(1) 
   
Attending veterinarian and adequate veterinary care (dealers and exhibitors). 
There was no written Program of Veterinary Care (PVC) available for review at the time of the relicense inspection. 
Although the applicant did have a formal arrangement with a part-time veterinarian who was the Attending Veterinarian 
(AV) and animals have been receiving veterinary care, this veterinarian is no longer willing to function as the AV. 
 
Per this Section, each dealer shall have an Attending Veterinarian (AV) who shall provide adequate veterinary care to its 
animals in compliance with this section and the dealer shall employ an AV under formal arrangements. In the case of a 
part-time Attending Veterinarian or consultant arrangements, the formal arrangements shall include a written Program of 
Veterinary Care and regularly scheduled visits to the premises of the dealer. The applicant needs to establish formal 
arrangements with a veterinarian who will serve as the AV and provide a PVC. 
 
This is a 1st Re-License Inspection for a Class “B” license. The inspection and exit briefing were conducted with the 
applicant. 
 
All items must be in compliance within 2 more inspection(s) or by 7/4/26 (60 days counted from 1st RL Inspection) or the 
applicant will forfeit the license fee and must wait 6 months to reapply. Please contact your inspector to schedule your 
next Re-License Inspection. 
 
Regulated activity can continue under your existing USDA license until it expires on 6/5/26.
